result:
## Findings

**Method note (important):** In this build's `compile_commands.json`, `os_unix.c`'s traced `os_malloc()`/`os_free()` (lines 677/724) are gated behind `#ifdef WPA_TRACE`, which is **not** defined for this compile config. `hover` on a call site (e.g. `eap_fast_common.c:101`) confirms `os_malloc` actually expands to the macro `#define os_malloc(s) malloc((s))` in `os.h`. Because it resolves to a macro, not a function, clangd's call-hierarchy (`prepareCallHierarchy`/`incomingCalls`) doesn't apply â€” macros have no call graph. So I enumerated call sites via `grep` (26 occurrences, cross-checked and reconciled against the raw count) and read each caller's full function body to verify exit paths.

All 26 `os_malloc()` call sites in `src/eap_common/*.c` are correctly matched with `os_free()` on every exit path (success and error). No leaks found.

| File:line | Function | Pattern |
|---|---|---|
| eap_fast_common.c:101 | `eap_fast_derive_key` | alloc-failâ†’return NULL (nothing to free); prf-failâ†’`os_free`+return NULL; successâ†’return buffer (ownership transferred to caller) |
| eap_ikev2_common.c:31 | `eap_ikev2_derive_keymat` | freed on error path (39) and success path (42) |
| eap_gpsk_common.c:140 | `eap_gpsk_derive_keys_helper` | freed on gkdf-fail (157) and success (160) |
| eap_gpsk_common.c:298 | `eap_gpsk_derive_keys` | alloc-failâ†’return; freed unconditionally at 336 regardless of switch outcome |
| eap_gpsk_common.c:371 | `eap_gpsk_derive_mid_helper` | freed on gkdf-fail (389) and success (392) |
| eap_gpsk_common.c:442 | `eap_gpsk_derive_session_id` | alloc-failâ†’return; freed unconditionally at 469 |
| eap_pwd_common.c:159 | `compute_password_element` | `prfbuf` initialized to `NULL`; all `goto fail` paths converge on shared cleanup that unconditionally calls `os_free(prfbuf)` (281) â€” safe even if alloc never reached (`free(NULL)` is a no-op) |
| eap_pwd_common.c:297 | `compute_keys` | alloc-failâ†’return -1; freed on both hash-init failures (307, 324) and after last use on success (331); no later return path re-touches `cruft` |
| eap_eke_common.c:350 | `eap_eke_derive_key` | freed on prf-fail (359) and success (362) |
| eap_eke_common.c:484 | `eap_eke_derive_ke_ki` | freed on prf-fail (492) and success (501) |
| eap_eke_common.c:525 | `eap_eke_derive_ka` | freed on prf-fail (540) and success (543) |
| eap_eke_common.c:570 | `eap_eke_derive_msk` | freed on prf-fail (586) and success (589) |
| eap_sim_common.c:178 | `eap_sim_verify_mac` | single exit; freed at 199 before return |
| eap_sim_common.c:373 | `eap_sim_verify_mac_sha256` | single exit; freed at 394 before return |
| eap_sim_common.c:946 | `eap_sim_parse_encr` | freed on decrypt-fail (952) and parse-fail (962); success returns buffer (ownership transferred, factory function) |
| eap_sake_common.c:328 | `eap_sake_compute_mic` | single exit; freed at 372 before return |
| ikev2_common.c:404 | `ikev2_derive_auth` | freed on prf-fail (420) and success (423) |
| ikev2_common.c:496 | `ikev2_decrypt_payload` | freed on decrypt-fail (502) and bad-padding (510); success returns buffer (ownership transferred) |
| ikev2_common.c:651 | `ikev2_derive_sk_keys` (`keybuf`) | freed on prf-fail (657) and unconditionally after all sub-derivations (718) |
| ikev2_common.c:663/671/679/687/695/703/711 | `ikev2_derive_sk_keys` (`keys->SK_d/ai/ar/ei/er/pi/pr`) | stored into `keys` struct (not local temps); if any allocation fails, `ikev2_keys_set()` check at 720 fails and `ikev2_free_keys()` (721) frees all seven fields (safe â€” `os_free(NULL)` no-ops for the ones never allocated or already NULL); on full success all seven are retained and freed later by the caller via `ikev2_free_keys()` |

No mismatched or missing `os_free()` was found. The only "unfreed" cases are three deliberate factory-style returns (`eap_fast_derive_key`, `eap_sim_parse_encr`, `ikev2_decrypt_payload`) plus the `ikev2_derive_sk_keys` struct-field allocations, all of which intentionally transfer ownership to the caller rather than leaking.
